articles.title: Come, Thou Glorious Day of Promise
articles.author: A. Alex. Neibaur
articles.complete_text:
Come, thou glorious day of promise,
Come and spread thy cheerful ray,
When the scattered sheep of Israel
Shall no longer go astray;
When hosannas,
With united voice they'll cry.

Lord, how long wilt Thou be angry;
Shall Thy wrath forever burn?
Rise, redeem Thine ancient people,
Their transgressions from them turn.
King of Israel,
Come and set Thy people free.

O, that soon Thou would'st to Jacob,
Thy enlivening Spirit send!
Of their unbelief and mis'ry
Make, O Lord, a speedy end.
Lord, Messiah!
Prince of Peace o'er Israel reign.
articles.notes: This is a complete hymn/poem by A. Alex. Neibaur with three stanzas, appearing in the contents but the full text is included in this misc file. It's substantial religious poetry content that should be a standalone file.

misc_summary: Contains table of contents, publication information (organ of Relief Society, subscription rates, mailing address), and extensive advertisements for local Salt Lake City businesses including ice cream, electrical services, optometry, laundry, music records, insurance, clothing, furniture, and food products.
